Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ＫＢ警備会社　社長室
English: October 13 KB Security CEO's Office

Japanese: ここが殺人現場、か‥‥
English: So I guess this is where it all went down, huh...?

Japanese: さすが警備会社の社長室だねー。 カベが厚そうだよ。
English: The walls in here look thick... just like you'd expect in a CEO's office.

Japanese: （それはカンケイないだろう‥‥）
English: (What has that got to do with anything...?)

Japanese: おっ、アンタたち！
English: Hey! It's you guys!

Japanese: あ‥‥イトノコ刑事。
English: Oh, it's Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: アンタたち、今日は サイナンだったッスねー。
English: Today was a real train wreck for you guys, huh?

Japanese: ほんとッスよ！　あの検事さんに、 うまくやられちゃって‥‥
English: Sure was, pal! That prosecutor made real fools out of us...

Japanese: ちょっぴり、同情するッス。
English: Yeah... I feel for you.

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。めずらしいですね。 いつものイトノコさんなら、
English: Wow, that's not like you at all... I thought you'd be more like...

Japanese: いいきみッス！　ざまーみろッス！ サイコーッス！　わははははッス！
English: "Oh, that was great! You guys got what you deserved, pal! Ho ho ho ho!"

Japanese: ‥‥みたいな感じなのに。
English: Or something to that effect.

Japanese: そ、そんなにイヤな 感じッスか？　自分‥‥。
English: D-Do I really sound like that to you, pal?

Japanese: ええ！　それはもう。
English: If the Gumshoe fits...

Japanese: ううううう‥‥とにかく。
English: Um... Well, anyway...

Japanese: 自分のシゴトを一生懸命やる 人間を、自分は認めるッス。
English: The point is I can tell when someone puts their heart into their jobs.

Japanese: そして‥‥それがウラ目に出た人間 に、自分は同情しちゃうッス。
English: And I can sympathize when things don't go your way.

Japanese: ‥‥刑事のシゴトも、 そんなコトばっかりッスから。
English: ...Sometimes, I feel like "wrong" is the only way things go for us detectives.

Japanese: 意外にいいヒトなんだ、 イトノコさん。
English: Wow, I had no idea Detective Gumshoe was such a nice guy.

Japanese: （なんでもいいけど、今なら 情報を集めやすそうだな‥‥）
English: (Now if this little love-fest is over, maybe we can start investigating...)

Japanese: お、こんなところにボタンが。 どれどれ‥‥
English: Oh, there's a button here. Let's see...

Japanese: ビビビイイイイイイーーーーーーッ
English: *BEEP BEEP BEEEEEEEEEP!!*

Japanese: あ！　ダメッス、 それを押しちゃあ！
English: Hey! Cut it out! Don't press that!

Japanese: あははは。イトノコさん、 ヨコっ飛びに走ってくるよ。
English: Ha ha ha! That was pretty funny. I never knew Detective Gumshoe could jump like that!

Japanese: なんですか、このボタン。
English: What is that button anyway?

Japanese: 非常ブザーッス！ パネルにキチンと書いてあるッス！
English: It's an emergency buzzer! It says it right there on the panel!

Japanese: あれ、ホントだ。 《非常ブザー》だって。
English: Oops, you're right! It's written right there!

Japanese: ダメだよ、なるほどくん。 ちゃんと読まなくちゃ。
English: Nick, how many times have I told you to read the instructions first!?

Japanese: 地下の警備室につながっていて、 ガードマンがやって来るッス。
English: This alarm's connected to the basement guard room. It's used to summon security up here.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ‥‥ もしかしたら、事件当夜も‥‥？
English: ...Really? Then it's possible on the night of the crime...

Japanese: あ！　そうだよ！　社長さん、 ハンニンに襲われたんですよね？
English: Ooh! So when the CEO was attacked...

Japanese: もしかしたら、ブザーを‥‥
English: ...do you think that maybe he pressed the buzzer...?

Japanese: 自分たちも、そう思ったッス。 で、ガードマンに聞いたッスが‥‥
English: Yeah, I thought about that, so I asked around down there...

Japanese: 事件当夜、ブザーは鳴らなかった らしいッス。それに‥‥
English: ...but they said that the buzzer never went off that night.

Japanese: ブザーのボタンには、 指紋が残っていないッス。
English: Also, we couldn't find any fingerprints on the buzzer.

Japanese: モチロン、被害者の毒島は 手袋なんか、していなかったッス。
English: Mr. Bullard, the victim, wasn't wearing any gloves, by the way. Just so you know.

Japanese: （非常ブザー‥‥。ガードマンの ハナシも、聞いたほうがいいな）
English: (Hmm... I think we'd better go and talk to that guard about this emergency buzzer.)

Japanese: 《非常ブザー》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Emergency Buzzer added to the Court Record.

Japanese: こりゃまた、ずいぶん ゴツい金庫だねー‥‥
English: Wow! This safe is unbelievable...

Japanese: 春美ちゃんなら ４人ぐらい入りそうだな。
English: I bet four Pearls would fit in there.

Japanese: それに‥‥いろんなキカイが くっついてるね。
English: And it's got a bunch of doohickeys attached to it.

Japanese: センサーみたいだ。震動に、 熱やらショックやら‥‥
English: It's pretty amazing alright. Motion sensors, heat sensors, weight sensors...

Japanese: へえええ。ほら、なるほどくん。 開ける開ける。
English: Hey, Nick. Come on! Let's open it and take a look!

Japanese: ‥‥こんなのが開けられたら、 怪人としてデビューするよ。
English: If I broke into one of these, wouldn't that set me down the path to hoodlumville?

Japanese: ううう‥‥この中に、死体が かくされていたんだね。
English: Ewww, think about it. A dead guy was laying in here all night.

Japanese: ちなみに、この金庫に 指紋は残っていないッス。
English: Yeah. Oh, by the way, don't bother asking about fingerprints. There were none.

Japanese: 事件当夜、だれが開けたんだろう ‥‥この金庫。
English: Well, somebody opened this safe on the night of the crime, right?

Japanese: え？　どういうこと？
English: Yeah, and so?

Japanese: この中に死体がかくされていた、 ってコトは‥‥
English: Well if Mr. Bullard's body was hidden in here...

Japanese: どちらかが開けたはずだろ？ ‥‥被害者か、犯人か。
English: ...it must mean that it was opened by either the killer or the victim, right?

Japanese: あ‥‥。そりゃそうだね。
English: Umm... Yeah, I guess so.

Japanese: クックックックッ‥‥ アンタにはイヤな情報があるッス。
English: Ho ho ho ho... I got some bad news for you, pal. Are you ready?

Japanese: な、なんですか？
English: Um, OK, shoot.

Japanese: 金庫を開けるには、ナニやら メンドウな手順があって‥‥
English: Opening this baby is no piece of cake...

Japanese: 開けられたのは、ほんの 数名しか、いないらしいッス。
English: There are only a few people who know how to open this safe, pal.

Japanese: そ、それが何か‥‥？
English: Y-Yeah, and...?

Japanese: 金庫を開けられた人物のアリバイは すでに、カクニン済みッス。
English: Everyone that knows how to open this safe had airtight alibis. I checked.

Japanese: たった１人をのぞいて。
English: Everyone except one, that is...

Japanese: だ、ダレですか？
English: I'm almost afraid to ask...

Japanese: ‥‥かつての警備隊長だった、 天杉　優作ッス。
English: Former security chief... Ron DeLite.

Japanese: えええええっ！ あの優作さんが、警備隊長！
English: Whaaaat!? Ron DeLite? HE was a security chief?

Japanese: （優作くんが‥‥金庫を 開けることができた、だと‥‥）
English: (Ron...? So he knew how to open the safe, huh...?)

Japanese: ま。これでまた１つ、 イヤな情報が増えたワケッスな。
English: Yeah... Sorry for raining on your parade there, pal.

Japanese: うわ。おっきな写真が 額に入ってるよ。
English: Wow! Look at that huge framed photo!

Japanese: ‥‥どんよりくもった空に、 うっそりと山がそびえている。
English: Tall mountains rising majestically against a dark and cloudy sky...

Japanese: 額の下に、写真の タイトルが書いてあるよ。
English: There's a title written at the bottom of the photo.

Japanese: 《我が人生、最高の初日の出》 ‥‥コレが？
English: "The Greatest Sunrise of My Life"... This is it? This was his best sunrise?

Japanese: よっぽどの雨ふらせオトコ だったんじゃないか。
English: Either this guy didn't get out enough or he had really rotten luck with the weather.

Japanese: ‥‥長生きすれば、もう少し マシな日の出を見られたのにね。
English: Maybe if he had lived a little longer, he would have seen some better days.

Japanese: 社長の座るデスクだろう。 意外にシンプルだな。
English: This must be the CEO's desk. It's a lot simpler than I would have thought.

Japanese: イスがふかふかだよ！ ちょっと座ってみようっと。
English: Hey, that looks like a super soft chair. Let me try it out, just for a second...

Japanese: ‥‥おお、ちょっぴり シャチョー気分。
English: Ooh. Nice... I feel just like a CEO.

Japanese: ホラ、そこのチミ。お茶と おだんご持ってきてくれたまえ。
English: Hey, you! Whip me up a cup of some really expensive import tea and some scones! Move it!

Japanese: ‥‥なーんて。
English: ...Ahhh, this is the life.

Japanese: そこのイス、被害者が死の直前に 座っていたッス。
English: Um, the victim sat in that chair just before he was brutally killed, you know.

Japanese: きゃわわわああああっ！
English: EEEEEEEK!

Japanese: これまた、ぶ厚くて重い ファイルが置いてあるぞ‥‥
English: Check out this big, thick binder here...

Japanese: チカラ仕事はまかせた、 なるほどくん！
English: Leave the heavy lifting to me, Nick.

Japanese: （ファイルを調べるのが メンドウなだけじゃないか‥‥）
English: (Reading a file isn't exactly back-breaking work, just a little hard on the eyes...)

Japanese: ‥‥あっ！
な、なに？　なるほどくん。
English: Wh-What did you find out, Nick?

Japanese: このファイル‥‥警備のコトなんか ゼンゼン書いてないぞ。
English: This file... It's not about any sort of security operations or anything!

Japanese: これ、ぜんぶ‥‥怪人☆仮面マスク に関するデータだよ！
English: This huge file is all about Mask☆DeMasque! It's filled with info on him!

Japanese: え！　ど、どういうコト‥‥？
English: What!? What kind of info?

Japanese: 犯行現場のようすやら、手口やら ‥‥びっしり書きこまれている。
English: It's filled with incredibly detailed information about his methods and the crime scenes.

Japanese: ‥‥？　ね、なるほどくん。 この、最後のページ‥‥
English: Hey, Nick. Look at the last page!

Japanese: リストだね。ええと、最初は 《エマノンの涙・１０００万》
English: It's a list. Let's see... "Tear of Emanon... $100,000"

Japanese: ‥‥仮面マスクが盗み出した エモノのリスト、なのかな？
English: ...This looks like a list of all the treasures that Mask☆DeMasque stole.

Japanese: じゃあ、その《１０００万》 って数字は、エモノのお値段？
English: So then "$100,000" is the value of the stolen item?

Japanese: ‥‥それにしては、 ちょっと安い気もするなあ。
English: ...I don't know, that number sounds kind of low to me.

Japanese: （このリスト‥‥コッソリ、 写しておいたほうがいいな）
English: (I think I'd better secretly make a copy of this list.)

Japanese: 《毒島のリスト》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Kane's List added to the Court Record.

Japanese: なんか、ロッカーみたいな モノが並んでいるよ。
English: These look like some kind of bookshelf/rolling cabinet hybrid.

Japanese: ‥‥ぐぐぐ。 あ、開けられないッ！
English: ...Nnnnnf! I can't get in between these two shelves!

Japanese: アオスジ立ててもムダだよ。 キカイで制御してるみたいだ。
English: Don't strain yourself trying. It looks like the shelves are controlled by a special panel.

Japanese: １つだけ、開いているね。 ‥‥これ、ファイルだよ。
English: So I guess it's one shelf at a time, huh? Looks like they're filled with bunches of files.

Japanese: （依頼された警備のデータが びっしり記録されている）
English: Yeah, files filled with data about security jobs they were hired to handle.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ、つまんないの。
English: It'd be a good night's reading. ...If you've got insomnia.

Japanese: もっとこう、ワクワクするような コトが書いてあればいいのにね。
English: I was hoping for something a little bit more exciting... Like UFOs or something.

Japanese: このロープ‥‥
English: This rope...

Japanese: 金庫にしまおうとしたとき、 はみ出しちゃったのかな？
English: You think it fell out of the safe when it was opened?

Japanese: ‥‥そうじゃないと思うよ。
English: ...I don't think so.

Japanese: じゃあ、やっぱり‥‥？
English: So, you mean...?

Japanese: うん。被害者の死体のカタチを ロープでなぞってあるんだよ。
English: Yeah, I think this string shows where and how the corpse was lying.
Y-Y-You mean...

Japanese: 被害者のヒト‥‥金庫を閉めるとき 挟まって死んじゃったのかな。
English: The victim... H-He was killed by being crushed by the safe door?

Japanese: （‥‥マジメに 言ってるのだろうか‥‥）
English: (Sh-She can't be serious... Can she...?)

Japanese: イトノコさん。事件のコト、 教えてください！
English: Detective Gumshoe. Tell us what you know about the murder!

Japanese: ううん、そッスねー。 ちょっと、それは立場的に‥‥
English: Umm, OK. But the thing is, I'm really not supposed to...

Japanese: あたしたち、 一生懸命やったんですよ！
English: Hey, come on! What about how we "put our hearts into our work"?

Japanese: それなのに‥‥それが ウラ目に出ちゃって‥‥
English: Things are really "working against us" right now and we need help...!

Japanese: わ、わかったッス！　教えるッス！ だから、泣いちゃダメッス！
English: OK, OK! I'll tell you! Just don't start crying on me, OK pal?

Japanese: うん。あたし、泣かないッス。
English: OK. I won't cry on you, pal.

Japanese: 毒島　黒兵衛（ぶすじまくろべえ） ‥‥それが、被害者の名前ッス。
English: The victim's name is Kane Bullard.

Japanese: ＫＢ警備の社長で、それはそれは アブラぎったオヤジさんッス。
English: He was the CEO of KB Security and a pretty big fella in his own right.

Japanese: 社長の死体が発見されたのは、 今朝の９時ごろッスが‥‥、
English: His corpse was discovered at 9:00 this morning...

Japanese: 殺害されたのは、９月１２日の 午前１時ごろッス。
English: His estimated time of death was 1:00 in the morning on October 12th.

Japanese: この部屋にあった置物で ガツンと殴られたのが、死因ッス。
English: Cause of death was blunt force trauma to the head. Probably an object in this room.

Japanese: （‥‥仮面マスクがツボを盗んだ 時間とピッタリ、か‥‥）
English: (It happened at exactly the same time that Mask☆DeMasque was stealing the urn, huh...)

Japanese: 証拠品《解剖記録》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Autopsy Report added to the Court Record.

Japanese: どうして、まる１日も死体が 発見されなかったんですか？
English: So why did it take almost an entire day to discover the body?

Japanese: やむを得なかったッス。
English: There's a good explanation for that one.

Japanese: ブスジマの死体は‥‥そこの 大金庫の中に入っていたッスから。
English: Bullard's body was stashed away inside the safe.

Japanese: 大金庫‥‥ （たしかに、デカい金庫だな）
English: Safe...? (Well, it is pretty big...)

Japanese: 社長に連絡が取れず、今朝 金庫を開けてみたら‥‥
English: Nobody had heard from him, and when they opened the safe this morning, out he came...

Japanese: 死体が転がりだした、と。 （あのロープが、死体の跡だな）
English: Oh, so the body fell out... (That white string must be the shape from when he fell out.)

Japanese: とりあえず‥‥ムスジマさんの 情報、もっとほしいね。
English: I think we still need some more information about Mr. Billiard...

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥。そもそも、 名前、まちがっておぼえてるし）
English: (Maybe you could start by getting the man's name right.)

Japanese: それで、あの‥‥ 仮面マスクは、どうなりました？
English: So, umm... What happened to Mask☆DeMasque?

Japanese: 留置所で、うらめしげな ヒメイを上げてるッス。
English: He's at the detention center screaming like a madman...

Japanese: “も一度、調べてくださーーい！” ‥‥って。
English: "Investigate me agaaaaain!" he keeps yelling.

Japanese: いやいや！　怪人の正体は そっちじゃないでしょう。
English: Ah, no no. I didn't mean him. He's not the real thief anyway, right?

Japanese: ああ。探偵の星威岳　哀牙ッスか。 クックックックッ‥‥
English: Oh, you mean that "Detective" Atmey? Ho ho ho ho ho...

Japanese: いいきみッス！　ざまーみろッス！ サイコーッス！　わははははッス！
English: Oh, that was great! That guy got what he deserved! Ho ho ho ho!

Japanese: ‥‥いつもの調子が出てきたよ、 なるほどくん。
English: Now that's the Detective I know and love.

Japanese: 怪人の予告状が届くと、 かならずあの探偵が現れたッス。
English: Think about it... Atmey was always around when a calling card showed up.

Japanese: でも‥‥カンジンの犯行の瞬間、 探偵はいつも、姿を消していた！
English: But he always mysteriously disappeared when the heists took place!

Japanese: 『現場の近くに、かくれていた』 と、ヤツは言っていたッスが‥‥
English: "I was hiding at the crime scene"... Heh! Yeah right!

Japanese: なんのコトはないッス！ アイツ自身が怪人だったッス！
English: That's the lamest thing I've ever heard! That's how you just knew he was the thief!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥彼自身が怪人なら、 エモノを取り戻すのも自由自在だ。
English: That would explain how he was able to retrieve the stolen item he keeps bragging about.

Japanese: 自分のカオを売るために、 そんなコトまでするとは‥‥
English: Yeah, he just did that to make himself look like a great detective. That's all.

Japanese: あ。‥‥ただ、 １回目の犯行だけは‥‥
English: ...But there's this one thing I can't figure out about his first heist...
His first heist...?

Japanese: 《エマノンの涙・盗難事件》ッス。 このときは、目撃者がいたッス。
English: Yeah, the "Tear of Emanon" case. There was a witness on that one.

Japanese: 目撃者‥‥？
English: A witness...?

Japanese: いちおう、新聞記事を 切りぬいておいたッスけど‥‥
English: Here, I saved the newspaper clipping...

Japanese: 怪人もタイホされちまったッスから ‥‥アンタにやるッス。
English: Since the thief is already under arrest, you guys can keep it.

Japanese: あれ。この警備員さん‥‥ どこかで見たこと、ない？
English: Hey, this guard here... Haven't I seen him somewhere before?

Japanese: （小さくてよく見えないけど、 言われてみれば‥‥）
English: (It's pretty small so it's kind of hard to see, but now that she mentions it...)

Japanese: 《新聞記事》の切りぬきを 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Newspaper Clipping added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ヒドいですよ、あの検事さん！
English: Ooh! That prosecutor! I really don't like that guy!

Japanese: あたしたちが見つけた 証拠を使って、優作さんを‥‥
English: The way he used our own evidence to do that to Mr. DeLite...

Japanese: アンタたちにダメージを与えるため ワザとあんなコトをした‥‥
English: Yeah, I think he did it that way just 'cause he knew it'd hurt more...

Japanese: そう考えるべきッスね。
English: That's what my gut tells me anyway.

Japanese: いったい‥‥ナニモノなんですか？ あのコーヒー好きは‥‥
English: So who is that java addicted, masked maniac anyway?

Japanese: ゴドー検事は、ナゾのミステリーに つつまれているッス。
English: Prosecutor Godot? He's quite the enigma, huh?

Japanese: じつは自分も、 今まで聞いたコトがなかったッス。
English: The thing is, pal... I never even heard of the guy before.

Japanese: ある日トツゼン、風とともに 検事局にやってきたッス。
English: He just showed up one day at the prosecutor's office. ...Came outta nowhere.

Japanese: 『検事として初めての事件だ』 ‥‥って、言ってましたよね。
English: That's right, he said this was his first case as a prosecutor!

Japanese: たしかに、記録の上では そうみたいッス。‥‥しかしッ！
English: And it's true... According to the records, anyway. But!

Japanese: ありゃア、シロートじゃねッス。 ぱきぱきのプロ、ッス。
English: No way he's an amateur. He's an iceman in court, a maverick that give me goosebumps.

Japanese: ぱきぱき‥‥
English: Goosebumps...? You?

Japanese: 自分も気になって、聞いて まわってみたッスが‥‥
English: Yeah. I knew something was off about him, so I asked around...

Japanese: ダレも知らないし、 教えてくれないッス。
English: Nobody would talk to me... They all just turned the other way.

Japanese: 人気なさそうですもんね。 ‥‥イトノコさん。
English: Poor Detective Gumshoe... I had no idea you were so unpopular...

Japanese: い、イヤ。 ‥‥そういうイミでは。
English: Ah, no. That's not what I meant...

Japanese: （ゴドー検事‥‥ぼくを 知ってるみたいだったけど‥‥）
English: (That Godot guy acts like he knows me and has a grudge against me...)

Japanese: （何か、ヒミツが ありそうだな‥‥）
English: (I get the feeling he's hiding some kind of dark secret...)

Japanese: ブスジマさんのこと、 詳しく聞かせてもらえますか？
English: Can you tell us some more about Mr. Bullard?

Japanese: ＫＢ警備のシャチョーさん、か。 そもそも、どんな会社なんですか？
English: He was the CEO of KB Security, right? What kind of company is it anyway?

Japanese: 契約したビルに、警備チームを ハケンする会社ッス。
English: Well, the company basically sends security teams out to buildings to guard them.

Japanese: シゴトがら、警備上の機密に触れる 機会が多かったッス。それに‥‥
English: Mr. Bullard must've had the chance to learn a lot of secrets doing this kinda work.

Japanese: それに？
English: Oh? And?

Japanese: なんというか‥‥被害者は、 カネに目がなかったッス。
English: And... I don't know how to put this, but the guy was kind of a moneygrubber.

Japanese: あ。あたしも！　目がないッスよ、 おカネには。
English: Really? Me too! I just love money! I can't ever get enough!

Japanese: （そんなに身を乗り出すなよ‥‥）
English: (Please stop leaning in towards me like that... You aren't getting to my wallet.)

Japanese: かなり、アクどいことをして、 もうけていたみたいッス。
English: Anyway, it looks like he did some pretty shifty stuff to earn his millions.

Japanese: あ、あたし、アクどくもないし、 もうけてもいないッス！
English: Oh! So that's my problem! I think I need to be shiftier!

Japanese: （そんなに身を引くなよ‥‥）
English: (Lemme go already...)

Japanese: シゴトの合間に、いろんな会社の ヒミツをのぞいて、売買したり。
English: Apparently he was involved in selling trade secrets between rival companies.

Japanese: ううん‥‥ワルモノですね、 そんなヤツ。
English: Ooh, that's pretty dirty and underhanded.

Japanese: ジツは、仮面マスクの 警備を担当したこともあるッス。
English: Yeah. Oh! KB Security used to head security operations against Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: え。そうなんですか！
English: What? Really?

Japanese: それで、ミゴトにシッパイして‥‥
English: Yeah, and after screwing up so many times...

Japanese: 業界で、かなり 信用を失っていたみたいッスねー。
English: ...the company's reputation really took a nosedive.

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥優作くんを脅迫 していたのは、毒島か‥‥）
English: (So it really was Bullard who sent Ron the blackmail letter, huh...?)

Japanese: ほら。弁護士のバッジです。
English: Here, take a look at my attorney's badge.

Japanese: まだ、そんなちっぽけな バッジにこだわってるッスか！
English: Are you still showing that cheap little thing off?

Japanese: やっぱり男は、 ダマって警察手帳ッス。
English: A real man keeps his mouth shut... and carries a suave police badge, pal.

Japanese: いッスか！　言っちゃダメッスよ。 エンブレムのコト。
English: Remember! Don't tell anyone about that emblem. OK, pal?

Japanese: この予告状‥‥警察には 届かないんですか？
English: Does the thief ever send these to the police?

Japanese: ザンネンながら、いつも被害者の ところだけッス。
English: No, always to the victims.

Japanese: これまでの被害者は、警察に 助けを求めてきたッスが‥‥
English: Up until now, the victims have always come to us looking for help though.

Japanese: ‥‥今回は、 そうじゃなかったんですか？
English: ...That's not what happened this time, you mean?

Japanese: ま、そッスな。
English: You got that right, pal.

Japanese: いッスか！　もしアンタたちが 怪人の予告状を受け取ったら‥‥
English: Listen up! If you guys ever get one of these calling cards...

Japanese: ちゃんと、ワレワレに 届けるッスよ！
English: ...I want you to come to us right away! Got me?

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: ...Yeah, sure.

Japanese: あの、これなんですけど。
English: Umm, so about this...?

Japanese: お。サンキュッス。 ちょうどノドが痛かったッス。
English: Hey! Thanks, pal! How did you know I had a sore throat anyway?

Japanese: ‥‥ぱく。
English: ...*chomp*

Japanese: きゃわああ！ ナニするんですかッ！
English: Hey! What are you doing!?

Japanese: ぎゃあ！
English: Ack!

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっとキレイだから、 キャンデーかと思ったッス。
English: Sorry, that thing's all smooth and shiny, so I thought it was a piece of candy or something.

Japanese: こんなデカいの、よく 口に入りますね。
English: I can't believe you ate the whole thing in one go...

Japanese: あやうく、飲みこむところ だったッス。
English: I almost swallowed it too, pal...

Japanese: ううう‥‥あたしの 勾玉があ‥‥
English: Ohh... My poor Magatama...

Japanese: 仮面マスクめ、なんで こんなボロっちい‥‥
English: I still got no idea why Mask☆DeMasque would go after a broken old pot like...

Japanese: じろり。
English: *glare*

Japanese: ‥‥こ、こんなアジのある ツボを盗んだッスかねえ‥‥。
English: Ah, I mean this tasteful, old family heirloom...

Japanese: そうですね。値打ちがゼロの ものが狙われたのは‥‥
English: Good point. Why go after a worthless piece of junk like...

Japanese: じろり。
English: *glare*

Japanese: いいイミで値打ちがゼロのものが 狙われたのは、初めてですよね。
English: I, um... I mean worthless in a good way. I mean, this is a first for the guy...

Japanese: （霧緒さんは、予告状が届く前に 哀牙に警備を依頼していた）
English: (Adrian Andrews hired Atmey as security before the calling card even came...)

Japanese: （‥‥それが、ナニか 関係あるのかもしれないな‥‥）
English: (I wonder if that has anything to do with how things played out...?)

Japanese: ケッキョク、アイガ探偵の後ろ アタマは殴られなかったのかな？
English: So Detective Atmey wasn't hit in the back of the head after all?

Japanese: そッスね。哀牙が怪人だった ワケッスから。
English: That's right. Because he was the real thief!

Japanese: “キゼツさせられた”と 偽装するために‥‥、
English: He wanted it to look like he was knocked out...

Japanese: くにゃっ、と 曲げておいたッスね、きっと。
English: So he bent it on purpose. I'd stake my reputation on it.

Japanese: ううう‥‥そんなコトのために、 お宝を曲げちゃうなんて‥‥
English: Grrr... What kind of a creep would ruin an antique for that...?

Japanese: 防犯カメラが撮影した怪人‥‥ エンブレムがありませんよね。
English: The thief in the photo taken by the security camera is missing his broach...

Japanese: そッス。‥‥ただ、写真自体に 細工のアトはないッスから‥‥
English: Yup. The thing is, there's no evidence that the photo was tampered with.

Japanese: コイツが午前０時５８分に 撮影されたのは、たしかッス。
English: It was definitely taken at 12:58 AM.

Japanese: （今日の逆転‥‥消えたエンブレム のムジュンから始まった）
English: (I turned the tables at the trial today with the mystery of this missing broach.)

Japanese: （これって、 グーゼンなのかな‥‥？）
English: (I wonder if there's something more to this missing piece of costume jewelry?)

Japanese: この脅迫状ですけど‥‥
English: About this blackmail letter...

Japanese: うーん。自分も考え中ッス。
English: Yeah, I've been thinking a lot about it, too, pal.

Japanese: 毒島　黒兵衛が、なぜ 天杉　優作を脅迫していたか‥‥？
English: Why would Kane Bullard have been blackmailing Ron DeLite anyway...?

Japanese: 優作くん‥‥ムカシ、ＫＢ警備で はたらいていたんですよね？
English: Well, Mr. DeLite used to work at KB Security, right?

Japanese: やめた理由って、なんですか？
English: But what was it that made him quit?

Japanese: あ、なるほど！　それが “キョーハク”の理由なのかも！
English: Nick, that's it! Maybe that's the same reason he's being blackmailed!

Japanese: それは‥‥今、取り調べ中ッス。
English: Well... I'm in the middle of investigating that right now.

Japanese: なんだ。
English: Oh... Alright.

Japanese: 天杉　優作が、事件のあった時間に ＫＢ警備にいた証拠ッスな。
English: This is proof that Ron DeLite was here at the crime scene when the murder happened.

Japanese: （サイフと‥‥ そして、キーカード）
English: (His wallet... and this key card, huh...)

Japanese: もう少し、優作さんに聞いてみた ほうがいいね。
English: I think we need to ask Mr. DeLite some more about those two things.

Japanese: あの、これなんですけど‥‥
English: Umm, so about this...

Japanese: なんスか、それ。
English: Huh? What's that?

Japanese: あ、ちょっと待って 真宵ちゃん！
English: Hey! Wait a minute, Maya!

Japanese: なに？　なるほどくん。
English: What's wrong?

Japanese: ‥‥ダメだよ。ぼくたちが 勝手に書き写したデータだぞ。
English: We copied that data without permission... Don't show it to him.

Japanese: 見せたら怒られるよ。
English: He might get mad at us.

Japanese: どうしたッス？
English: So? What are you two whispering about?

Japanese: あ‥‥。やっぱりいいです。 あたしのおこづかい帳ですから。
English: Errr... Oh, it's nothing. It's just my billfold.

Japanese: ずいぶん、ぶ厚いッスねー‥‥
English: That's a pretty thick billfold you got there, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥よけい見たくなったッス。
English: ...Now I really want you to show it to me.

Japanese: ヤツの初めての犯行だったので、 自分もアマく見ていたッス。
English: This was his first heist, and I guess I underestimated him.

Japanese: すぐ捕まると思って、目撃者の データをなくしちゃったッス。
English: I was sure I was going to get him, so I was careless and lost the info on the witness.

Japanese: “目撃者”って、この写真の ガードマンですか？
English: By witness, do you mean the guard in this photo?

Japanese: そのワカモノと探偵が協力して 怪人を追ったらしいッスが‥‥
English: Yeah, he and the detective worked together to try catching Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: 逃がしちゃったんですねー、 仮面マスク。
English: But, he got away anyway, huh?

Japanese: その、ガードマン‥‥
English: That guard...

Japanese: なんか最近、どこかで 見たような気もするッスが‥‥
English: I got the weird feeling that I've seen him around lately.

Japanese: イトノコさん。 これなんですけど‥‥
English: Detective Gumshoe... About this right here...

Japanese: ザンネンッスが‥‥アタマに入る 情報には、限界があるッス。
English: Sorry... It's hard to believe, but there's a limit to how much my brain can hold.

Japanese: 目と耳は２個あるッスが、 ノーミソは１個ッスからねえ。
English: I got two ears and two eyes, but I only got one brain.

Japanese: （目や耳とちがって、ノーミソは 見えないから‥‥）
English: (I can see your eyes and the ears...)

Japanese: （はたしてホントに１個あるのか、 カクニンしにくいよな‥‥）
English: (...but the jury is still out on the brain thing...)

Japanese: 言いたいコトがあったら、 口に出そうよ、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick, if you have something to say, just come out and say it.

Japanese: サエないカオッスな。
English: Pretty goofy looking face, if you ask me.
Hey...!

Japanese: 持ち歩かないほうが いいんじゃないかな、それ。
English: What are you doing carrying that around with you anyway? It's not like we ever need it.

Japanese: （ホントだな‥‥）
English: (She has got a point...)

Japanese: 見慣れたカオッスが‥‥
English: You're such a familiar face to me now, you know?

Japanese: よく考えたら、いちおう 被害者なんスねー、今回は。
English: And now that I think about it, you're the real victim in this case, right?

Japanese: ‥‥どうにも ピンと来ないッスけど。
English: But for some strange reason, it's hard to think of you as a victim, pal.

Japanese: そッスよ！　秘宝を 盗まれちゃったんですから！
English: You're darn right I'm the victim! That was our most precious treasure!

Japanese: ヒホー、ねえ‥‥
English: Ha ha. You know, pal...

Japanese: やっぱり、どうにも ピンと来ないッス。
English: I think I know why I can't think of you as a victim, ever.

Japanese: 見るからに“オニ刑事”って 感じッスよねー。
English: I know I look a little rough around the edges.

Japanese: じつは最近、ますます 刑事っぽさがアップしてきたッス。
English: But the truth is, I'm a lot more "detective-like" than I used to be.

Japanese: へええ。どのへんがですか？
English: Really? Like what part?

Japanese: ‥‥コートの色あいとか。
English: ...Like the color of my coat.

Japanese: あ！　言われてみれば。
English: Ah! You're right!

Japanese: （コートの中身のほうは どうなんだろう‥‥）
English: (Yeah, but what about what's inside the coat...?)

Japanese: いやー、自分は最初っから アヤシイとニラんでいたッス！
English: I always knew there was something suspicious about this guy!

Japanese: 仮面マスクは、じつは あのクソ探偵ではないか、と！
English: And I was right! It turned out that DeMasque was really that "Ace Detective" in disguise!

Japanese: ホントですか‥‥？
English: You really thought that?

Japanese: う、ウソじゃねッス！ ほほ、ホントッス！
English: Huh? Of course! It's the stone-cold truth!

Japanese: ヤツを泳がせておけば、 いつかシッポを出す、と‥‥
English: Sooner or later, a guy like that always shows his true colors...

Japanese: ちょっと、泳がせすぎましたね。
English: Yeah, well I think we saw a little too much of his colors today...

Japanese: それは、その。 ‥‥反省点ッス。
English: Yeah... I gotta agree with you on that one.

Japanese: まあ、キノドクな 青年だったッスな。
English: I kind of feel sorry for him. He's kind of a sad young guy.

Japanese: いつも、タイホを 急ぎすぎるんですよ。
English: Yeah, and he seems to have a habit of always getting arrested prematurely.

Japanese: まあ、これはあくまでも 自分の意見ッスが‥‥
English: Well, it's just my personal opinion, but...

Japanese: 警察は、もっと慎重に ウラづけ捜査をするべきッスね。
English: I think the police should've investigated the whole thing more carefully.

Japanese: ‥‥大よろこびでタイホしたの、 イトノコさんだったよね。たしか。
English: But weren't YOU the one that arrested him with a giant goofy smile on your face...?

Japanese: ハナシが長くなるから、 そっとしておこう。
English: Come on, we don't have time to start with that. Let's just leave it alone.

Japanese: イトノコさん！　ムスジマさんの コト、聞かせてください！
English: Detective Gumshoe! Tell us more about Mr. Shane Bluebard!

Japanese: “ムスジマ”じゃなくて “ブスジマ”ッス。
English: That's "Kane Bullard", not "Shane Bluebard", pal.

Japanese: うーん‥‥。どうも今回の被害者、 インショーがウスいんだよねー。
English: Oh yeah... The victim in this case just doesn't make much of an impression on me.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥。午前中までは 真宵ちゃんだったもんな、被害者。
English: Well, you were the victim up until Mr. Bullard was found dead.

Japanese: しかたないッス。死体が発見された のが、今朝だったッスから。
English: Yeah, and his body wasn't discovered until this morning.

Japanese: 情報が足りないんです。 おねがいしますよ。
English: Anyway, we don't have enough information yet. Can you help us out?

Japanese: ううう‥‥自分も今、 コンランしてるッス。
English: Sorry... I'm actually a little confused myself.

Japanese: このままじゃ、なんでも 口走っちまいそうッス‥‥
English: For some reason, I'm just blabbing like an idiot right now...

Japanese: いい感じだね、なるほどくん。 詳しく聞いてみようよ！
English: OK, Nick! Now is our chance to get more info about the victim, so hurry up and ask!

Japanese: あ。‥‥綾里　千尋さんッスな。 キレイなヒト、だったッス。
English: Oh! That's Ms. Mia Fey! She was a truly beautiful person, pal.

Japanese: え‥‥あの。知ってるんですか？ お姉ちゃんのコト‥‥
English: Huh? You knew her? You knew my sis?

Japanese: 警察の関係者で、彼女のことを 知らない人間はいないッス。
English: Sure. Everybody in the police force knew her.

Japanese: ‥‥惜しいヒトをなくしたッス。
English: ...We all lost a great friend that day...

Japanese: な、泣いてなんかいねッス！ ギンバエが目に入っただけッス！
English: I-I just got something in my eye...! ...I'm not crying!

Japanese: ナゾのミステリーに つつまれているッス。
English: The guy is a real puzzle, if you ask me.

Japanese: アンタこそ、なんか 知らないッスか？　検事のコト。
English: Then maybe I should be asking you! Do YOU know something about him?

Japanese: 知ってるワケないでしょう。
English: Why would I know something?

Japanese: いやー。今日の法廷を聞いてたら、 何やらワケありに見えたッスよ。
English: Well, it sure sounded like you two knew each other from the way you acted today.

Japanese: ゴドー検事、なるほどくんのこと 知ってるみたいだったもんね。
English: It's Mr. Godot that acts like he knows Nick, not the other way around.

Japanese: （そう言われても‥‥ぼくには おぼえがないんだよな）
English: (Exactly. You'd think I'd remember someone like him...)

Japanese: ‥‥あやしいッス。
English: I still say the whole thing's a bit suspect.

Japanese: イトノコさん。 このヒトのことなんですけど。
English: Detective Gumshoe. What about this person?

Japanese: すまねッスが‥‥
English: Sorry, pal...

Japanese: 今、自分のアタマの中は 事件のコトでいっぱいッスから。
English: To be honest, my head is so full right now it feels like it's gonna burst.

Japanese: （“いっぱい”なのか “いっぱいいっぱい”なのか‥‥）
English: (Note to self: Keep hair away from Gumshoe's head.)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ＫＢ警備会社　警備室
English: October 13 KB Security Security Guard Office

Japanese: うわあ‥‥ なんと言うか、ものスゴいな。
English: Wow... This is really something else.

Japanese: さすが、本場の警備室は アヤシイねー、フンイキが。
English: For a security guard office, it sure doesn't feel very secure.

Japanese: （“ＫＢ警備の警備室”と いえば、ヤッパリ‥‥）
English: ("KB Security Guard"...! Uh oh, I just remembered... Larry might be...)

Japanese: よー、成歩堂じゃねえか！
English: Hey, Nick! Wassup!

Japanese: （ヤッパリ、コイツが いるワケか‥‥）
English: (Ugh! So he IS here...)

Japanese: おッ！　マヨイちゃんも いっしょじゃねえか！
English: Yo! How's it hanging, dude? And you got my sweet little Maya with you, too!

Japanese: こんにちは、ヤッパリさん！
English: Hi, Larry!

Japanese: くっそー。ヒトが死ぬ思いで はたらいてるってのに‥‥
English: Here I was working my fingers to the bone...

Japanese: いい気なモンだよなァ！ 昼間っからデートなんてよォ。
English: And in walks an angel! I've got no problems with a daytime date. It's all good!

Japanese: いやいや。今、ブスジマさんの 事件を調べてるんだよ。
English: No, that's not what we're here for. We're investigating the Bullard murder case.

Japanese: え。‥‥ああ！　そういや 弁護士だったっけ、オマエ。
English: ...Huh? Oh yeah! That's right! You're a lawyer, aren't you?

Japanese: （セツない‥‥）
English: (He's so hopelessly clueless.)

Japanese: あの事件なー。オレ、 イイ情報、知ってるんだぜ！
English: Well, if it's about the murder case, boy have I got some good info for you!

Japanese: え！　教えてもらえませんか！
English: Really!? What is it!?

Japanese: うーん‥‥マヨイちゃんになら、 教えてやってもいいかなあ。
English: Hmm... Well, I don't mind sharing with my sweet little Maya.

Japanese: あ、でも成歩堂はダメだぜ！
English: But Nick here is a different story!

Japanese: でも、ヤッパリさん‥‥ トモダチ、じゃないんですか？
English: But Larry, I thought you two were old school buddies...

Japanese: それはそれ。これはこれ、だぜ。
English: That was then and this is now.

Japanese: 細かい字で、 なにやら書いてある。
English: Something is written on this poster in fine print.

Japanese: 《警備員・鋼鉄のオキテ５カ条》 ‥‥スゴそうだね、なんか。
English: "A Guard's 5 Commandments" ...Wow, this sounds serious.

Japanese: ええと‥‥なになに。
English: Let's see what it says...

Japanese: 《その１・センパイの命令には ゼッタイ服従》
English: #1: Obey thy superior

Japanese: 《その２・センパイをうやまう》
English: #2: Respect thy superior

Japanese: 《その３・センパイには いつもエガオで》
English: #3: Smile at thy superior

Japanese: 《その４・センパイには キチンとアイサツ》
English: #4: Salute thy superior

Japanese: 《その５・センパイにはいつも おやつを買っておく》
English: #5: Buy donuts for thy superior upon command

Japanese: 《‥‥以上。 大センパイ・大場》
English: It's signed, "Wendy Oldbag, Head Supervisor".

Japanese: なんか、ミョーにハクリョクがある オバチャンでよ。
English: She's one tough old bird, let me tell you.

Japanese: さからうと、ホンモノの 光線銃を向けられるんだぜ。
English: Cross her and you come face-to-face with her real, genuine ray gun!

Japanese: ‥‥そいつはタイヘンだな。
English: ...Yeah, sounds scary alright.

Japanese: ま。今は彼女、旅行中でさ。 こうしてハネをのばしてるワケ。
English: Well, fortunately she's on vacation. That's why I'm so relaxed right now.

Japanese: そのモニターに、ビルのようすが 映るワケよ。ゼーンブ。
English: Those screens there show what's going on all over the building. Everywhere.

Japanese: それを、オレさまがじきじきに チェックするワケよ。ゼーンブ。
English: And it's my job to keep a steady eye on them. All of them.

Japanese: エラそうに言うなよ。
English: I wouldn't sound so smug if I were you.

Japanese: このテレビ、ふつうの番組は 映らないんですか？
English: Can you watch regular TV on these, too?

Japanese: （たしかにコイツなら、サボって テレビぐらい見そうだもんな）
English: (If anyone would sit here and watch TV instead of working, it's Larry.)

Japanese: マヨイちゃん。オレはプロだぜ。 ‥‥そんなの、映るわけないだろ。
English: Hey, Maya, I'm a pro. OK? Besides, you can't get regular TV on it.

Japanese: なんで“映らない”って 知ってるんだ？　矢張。
English: And how do you know that, Larry?

Japanese: だってオレ、まっ先に調べたから。 なんとかならねえかな、って。
English: Because that was my first bit of investigation, if you know what I mean.

Japanese: いや！　ちがうって！ プロとしてチェックしたんだよ！
English: I know what you're thinking! It was a professional investigation, alright!?

Japanese: このヘンも、タイヘンなことに なってますねー。
English: Wow! Take a look at these things here!

Japanese: あの。あの。ヤッパリさん。 なんですか？　このキカイ‥‥
English: Hey! Hey, Larry! What are they!?

Japanese: さあ‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: さ、“さあ”って‥‥
English: Did you say, "Hmm"?

Japanese: そんなの知らなくても、 警備員はつとまるからな。
English: Hey, man. It's not like I have to know what they are to do my job.

Japanese: ま。めずらしいインテリアだと オレは思ってるケド。
English: I... I always thought they were just some kind of decoration or something.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Oh boy...

Japanese: （なんでこんなオトコが 採用されたんだろう‥‥）
English: (How did this guy ever get a job here anyway...?)

Japanese: ‥‥あのジャケット、 お前のだよな、矢張。
English: ...Hey, Larry. That's your jacket, isn't it?

Japanese: そうだぜ。
English: That's right.

Japanese: あの。キカイのレバーに 引っかけてありますけど‥‥
English: Umm... Did you know you hung it right on top of some kind of lever?

Japanese: ああ。そのレバー、ゼーッタイ 倒すな、って言われててさあ。
English: Yeah, sure. I was told to never, ever, touch that lever. She scowled and huffed at me,

Japanese: 『トンでもないコトになる！』 なんて、コワいカオされちゃって。
English: "Something terrible will happen if you do! Got it, greenhorn?"

Japanese: ‥‥どうして、そんなアブない レバーにわざわざ上着を‥‥？
English: ...So why hang your jacket on such an important lever?

Japanese: だって、気になるじゃねえか。
English: Because it got me curious.

Japanese: 上着の重さでレバーが倒れれば、 それは事故ってヤツだからさ。
English: If the jacket's weight pulls the lever down, that's what they call an "accident".

Japanese: ナニが起こるか‥‥？ ちょっと、ゾクゾクするだろ？
English: Doesn't the suspense just kill you? Don't you want to know what'll happen? Huh?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ちょっと ゾクゾクするね、なるほどくん。
English: It's true... It's killing me, too! What about you, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥あるイミ、ね。
English: Yeah, but for a different reason...

Japanese: そこは、アイボーの席だぜ。
English: That's my partner's seat.

Japanese: アイボー‥‥？
English: Your... partner?

Japanese: うん。オレのセンパイで、 ヘンなオバチャンなんだけど。
English: Well, that's what I call her. She's my superior, actually. ...Kind of a weird old lady.

Japanese: あ、あの。キカイの上に お茶がこぼれてますよ！
English: Umm... There's tea spilled all over that machine, you know!

Japanese: あー、大丈夫だよ。
English: Oh, don't worry about it.

Japanese: オレなんか、こないだ コーヒー牛乳かけちゃったけど、
English: Just the other day, I spilled some chocolate milk on mine.

Japanese: まだ動いてるからな。 なかなか、よくできたキカイだぜ。
English: It still works fine, more or less. They really know how to build them, I guess.

Japanese: そこはオレの仕事場だぜ。 なんかこう、カッコイイだろ？
English: That's my workstation. Pretty cool, huh?

Japanese: モニターをしっかり見張って、 マイクでしっかり交信するワケ。
English: I keep a steady eye on the monitors and use that mic for communicating.

Japanese: いろんなキカイがありますね。 タイヘンだなー、こりゃ。
English: Look at all this equipment. It must be hard to operate.

Japanese: 大丈夫。この部屋のどこかに 説明書があるからな。
English: No biggie. I think there's an instruction manual somewhere in this room.

Japanese: “どこかに”‥‥？
English: "Somewhere"...?

Japanese: “説明書”‥‥？ 何か起こったら、どうするんだよ！
English: "Instruction manual"...? What are you going to do in an emergency?

Japanese: まずオレは、説明書を探す ところから始めるワケよ。
English: Well, I guess I'd start by calmly looking for the instruction manual.

Japanese: そのあいだに、オレのアイボーの オバチャンが、老眼鏡を探すワケ。
English: During that time, my partner, the old lady, would calmly look for her reading glasses.

Japanese: いわゆるチームワーク、 ってヤツだな。
English: That's what us security professionals call "teamwork".

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんと春美ちゃんを 置いておいたほうがマシだぞ）
English: (Even Maya and Pearls could run this place better.)

Japanese: どういうイミよ、それ！
English: Hey! What's that supposed to mean!

Japanese: それで？　“イイ情報”って なんだよ、矢張。
English: So what's this "good info" you were talking about, Larry?

Japanese: オレ、ガードマンよ。プロの。 タダで情報、流すワケねえだろ！
English: Hey! I'm a guard! A pro! I can't just give away information for free!

Japanese: （カネを払えばいいのか？ プロのガードマンって）
English: (He wants a bribe? I thought professionals were more, I don't know... HONEST!?)

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、真宵ちゃん。よろしく。
English: ...Can you talk to him, Maya?

Japanese: ヤッパリさん！　なんですか？ “イイ情報”って！
English: Larry! Tell us already! What's the "good info"!?

Japanese: いいね、そのシンケンなまなざし。 おニイさん、教えちゃおうかなー。
English: Hey, I like that! This kitten has got some claws! OK, you really want to know?

Japanese: うんうん。 教えちゃってくださいよ！
English: Yes, yes! So tell me!

Japanese: じつはさー、あの天杉　優作。 ここの社員だったっていうからさ。
English: OK. So the thing is... Ron DeLite was an employee here.

Japanese: トーゼン、調べてみたワケよ。 オレ、プロだし。ガードマンだし。
English: And naturally since I'm a pro, I looked into his background. Follow me?

Japanese: そうですよね！　プロですもんね！ ‥‥それで？
English: Yes, you're a pro! I follow you! Go on!

Japanese: 天杉　優作な。‥‥クビに なってるんだよ！　１年前に。
English: Well one year ago, Ron DeLite was fired!

Japanese: しかも、なんの予告もなく、 いきなりだぜ。
English: And there was no warning at all! It just happened all of a sudden!

Japanese: 小さい会社とはいえ、 ちょっとヒドいよなー。
English: I know this is a small company, but I think that was pretty awful.

Japanese: 何か、やっちゃったのかな。 “いきなりクビ”なんて。
English: I guess he must have done something bad to have gotten fired like that.

Japanese: 仕事サボって、女でもクドきに 行ったんじゃねえの？
English: Like maybe skipping out of work to go pick up hot chicks or something...

Japanese: （それは、おまえだろ‥‥）
English: (No, that's just you...)

Japanese: おまえ、事件当夜も この警備室にいたんだよな？
English: You were in this office when the murder took place, weren't you?

Japanese: そ、それがなんだよ？
English: Wh-Why do you say that?

Japanese: アルバイトだし、ややこしい コトはわからねえぜ。バカだし。
English: This is just a part-time job for me! And I can't operate the equipment! And I'm dumb!

Japanese: たとえアルバイトでバカだろうと、 警備のセキニンシャはおまえだろ。
English: Even if it is part-time and you are dumb, you're still in charge of security here!

Japanese: なんだよそれ！　オレにゼンブ 押しつけて、自分は知らんカオか！
English: Hey gimme a break! Don't try to pin the whole thing on me!

Japanese: キタネエぞ、成歩堂！
English: That's not fair, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥はい？
English: ...Huh?

Japanese: なるほどくん。セキニンは 追求しないほうがいいみたいだよ。
English: I don't think you can expect someone like him to take any responsibility.

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、あの晩 おまえはここにいたんだよな。
English: Anyway, the point is you were here that night, right?

Japanese: （イヤな予感がしたけど ‥‥ヤッパリ、か‥‥）
English: (Oh no, I knew something smelled bad, and it was the Butz after all...)

Japanese: ま、いわゆるアレよな。 それはそれ。これはこれ、だぜ。
English: Well, it's like I always say. That was then and this is now. OK?

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥ロックを 解除するしかないみたいだ）
English: (It looks like I'm going to have to break his Psyche-Lock after all.)

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Nnrgh! I've made too many mistakes!)

Japanese: 事件当夜のコトを 聞きたいんです、ヤッパリさん！
English: Larry, I want you to tell me about the night of the murder.

Japanese: ヤッパリ、問題はそれよなー。
English: Aw, man. Why do we have to talk about that?

Japanese: そ、そんなコトより、今夜の予定に ついて話そうぜ、マヨイちゃん！
English: Wh-Why don't we talk about what we're gonna do tonight instead, Maya!?

Japanese: （ダメだ‥‥サイコ・ロックを 解除しないと‥‥）
English: (Argh... I won't get anywhere if I can't break this Psyche-Lock...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんの勾玉を つきつけるんだったな‥‥）
English: (I think I have to present Maya's Magatama to get the ball rolling here...)

Japanese: ううう‥‥ヤッパリ‥‥ オレのせいなのか！
English: Oooh... I knew it...! It's all my fault!

Japanese: オレのせいで、シャチョーは‥‥ 死んじまったのかよおおおお。
English: It's my fault that the Boss was killed! My fault!!

Japanese: ヤッパリさん‥‥
English: Larry...

Japanese: でも！　しかたねえじゃねえか！ オレだって、ダイジな話が‥‥
English: There was nothing I could do! I have important issues to deal with too, man!

Japanese: いったい、あの晩 何があったんだ？
English: What happened that night anyway?

Japanese: え‥‥‥エミコが。
English: Aaah... My Donna happened.

Japanese: は？　えみこさん？
English: Huh? Your Donna?

Japanese: （なんだか、イヤな予感‥‥）
English: (I got a bad feeling about this...)

Japanese: 『どうしても今、お話ししたいの』 って、エミコが言うから‥‥
English: Yeah, my Donna called and said, "I have to talk to you right away..."

Japanese: それで、行ってみたら‥‥アイツの ヨコに、立ってやがったんだよ！
English: So I went to see her and... he was standing right there next to her!

Japanese: あの。ダレが‥‥？
English: Um, who was?

Japanese: 新しい彼氏だってよ！ 笑わせるじゃねえか！
English: Her new boyfriend! It was like some horrible joke!

Japanese: オレ、なんか知らんけど、 一発パンチされたワケよ！
English: Before I knew what was going on, the guy socked me right in the kisser!

Japanese: フツー、殴るのはこっちだろうが！ なあ、マヨイちゃんよ！
English: Normally I'm the one that does the punching! Isn't that right, Maya!?

Japanese: はああ‥‥
English: Y-Yeah...

Japanese: そ、そんなことで‥‥ 抜け出したのか？　警備室。
English: S-So is that why you left the security guard office?

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおお！ オレって‥‥オレってヤツぁ！
English: WAAAAAAH!! I'M SORRRRY! It-It-It's all my faaaaault!

Japanese: どこまでダメなんだ、成歩堂！ なるほどおよおおおぉぉぉ‥‥
English: H-How can I ever make up for it, Nick!? What can I do!? Whaaat!? Wah haa waa...

Japanese: ‥‥ユカにつっぷして 泣きはじめたよ。
English: He's curled up on the floor, crying like a baby.

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥
English: Oh boy...

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂！
English: ...Nick!

Japanese: 何か！　何かオレに できること、ねえか！
English: Is there anything I can do!? Anything! Just name it!

Japanese: オレ‥‥出なおすよ！ マジメにやりなおすからさァ！
English: I'll do whatever I have to do to make up for it! I swear I will!

Japanese: 矢張‥‥
English: Larry...

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。せっかくだし、 何か見てもらおうか。証拠品でも。
English: Hey Nick, as long as he's offering, why don't you show him the evidence we've got?

Japanese: （そうだな。さすがに１つぐらい ヤクに立つ情報があるかも‥‥）
English: (She's right. Maybe we'll get at least one bit of useful information from him...)

Japanese: なるほどおおおおおお‥‥
English: Niiiiiick...

Japanese: アルバイトでガードマン‥‥って、 どんな仕事なんだ？
English: So what is it like to be a part-time security guard?

Japanese: すげェタイヘンだぜ。‥‥まあ、 オレだからなんとかやってるケド。
English: Lemme tell you, it's tough. Well, you know me... I get by alright, I guess.

Japanese: まず、そこのモニターを 見張るワケよ。じーっと。
English: First, I have to keep my eye on those monitors. All the time.

Japanese: “もにたあ”？
English: Monitors?

Japanese: ビル中に監視カメラがセット されてるんだよ。
English: There are security cameras set in each room around the building.

Japanese: タイヘンな仕事だぜ！ なんてったって、目がつかれるし。
English: It's really hard! Sometimes I feel like my eyes are gonna fall out!

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Oh...

Japanese: で、アヤしいものを見つけたら、 すぐチームに連絡を入れるんだ。
English: And if I see something suspicious, I have to contact one of the teams.

Japanese: “ちいむ”？
English: What "teams"?

Japanese: この会社の警備チームだよ。 “意外とヤル”ってウワサだぜ。
English: The security teams for this company. They're supposedly the best in the business.

Japanese: ま。オレぐらいのプロになると、 少々のコトじゃ呼ばねえけど。
English: But I'm no amateur either, so if it's something small, I don't bother calling them.

Japanese: （ケッキョク、モニターを 見てるだけか‥‥）
English: (So in other words, you basically watch TV screens all day long...)

Japanese: あの‥‥あたしたち、さっき ブザーを鳴らしたんですけど‥‥
English: Umm, I accidentally pressed the buzzer earlier...

Japanese: ああ、聞いたよ。 マヨイちゃんだったんだ、アレ。
English: Yeah, I heard it. So that was you, huh?

Japanese: おまえ、警備員だろ。 なんで来なかったんだよ、社長室。
English: You're a security guard, aren't you? Why didn't you come to the CEO's office!?

Japanese: だって、ここは地下３階だぜ。 社長室は１階にあるから‥‥
English: Well, this is the 3rd floor of the basement. The CEO's office is on the 1st floor...

Japanese: とりあえず、待機したほうが いいかな、って。
English: I thought it would be a good idea to, um... adopt a wait and see approach.

Japanese: ホレ、今は刑事さんもいるしさ。 めんどくさかったワケじゃねえぜ！
English: Plus, there's a police detective here, right!? I just didn't think it was necessary!

Japanese: （ホントかよ‥‥）
English: (It's as if he's trying to win an award for the laziest person on the planet...)

Japanese: あの。 事件があった夜なんですけど。
English: Umm... Let's get back to talking about the night of the murder, OK?

Japanese: このブザー‥‥ホントに 鳴らなかったんですよね？
English: Is it true that the buzzer didn't go off that night?

Japanese: 記録は残ってるんだろ？ ちゃんと、調べたんだろうな。
English: There must be a record, right? You must have had a look at it, right?

Japanese: 当たり前だろ！　見まちがえる なんて、あり得ねえよ。
English: Of course I did! And I couldn't possibly have made a mistake either!

Japanese: いちおう、もう一度だけ 見てもらえますか？
English: Do you think you could take just one more look for me? Pretty please?

Japanese: ううーん。マヨイちゃんの たのみじゃ、しゃアねえなあ。
English: Oh, OK... I just can't say no to you, Maya.

Japanese: ‥‥どうかな、なるほどくん。
English: What do you think, Nick?

Japanese: たしかに、いくら矢張でも、 見まちがえるってコトは‥‥
English: He's probably right... I don't think even Larry could make a mistake like that...

Japanese: うごおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: WAAAAAAAAAAH!!

Japanese: ど、どうかしました？
English: Wh-What is it? What's wrong?

Japanese: 見まちがえてたよ、オレ！
English: I-I made a mistake!

Japanese: え！　そ、それで‥‥ どうなんですか？
English: Huh? B-But how...?

Japanese: あり得ねえ‥‥あり得ねえよ！
English: It can't be... It's impossible!

Japanese: それはいいから！　記録は？
English: OK, enough already! What about the records?

Japanese: あの晩‥‥１回だけど‥‥ 鳴ってるぜ。午前‥‥１時ごろだ。
English: That night... It went off just once... In the morning... At around 1 AM...

Japanese: ご‥‥午前１時って‥‥ サツジンがあった時間だよ！ 1 AM...?
English: Th-That's when the m-murder happened!

Japanese: えげげええェッ！　ホントかよォ！ ヤバいって！　あり得ねえって！
English: R-Really? Are you serious!? That's terrible! It can't be!

Japanese: 《非常ブザーの記録》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Buzzer Record added to the Court Record.

Japanese: おい、矢張。ちょっと 見てもらいたいモノが‥‥
English: Hey, Larry, there's something I want you to look at...

Japanese: あのさー、成歩堂。オレ、 シゴト中なワケよ‥‥プロとして。
English: Hey, Nick. I told you. I'm a pro. And you don't interrupt a pro when he's working.

Japanese: オマエとおしゃべりしてるヒマ、 ねえんだよな。ワルいけど。
English: I don't have time to sit and chat with you, alright?

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥
English: Oh, is that right...?

Japanese: あ！　もちろん、 マヨイちゃんはベツだぜ！
English: Oh! Heh heh, naturally I've got time for YOU though, Maya!

Japanese: （情報を聞き出すには‥‥ 思い知らせてやるしかない、か）
English: (We're gonna have to squeeze this guy if we want to get more info out of him, huh?)

Japanese: 大丈夫だって、成歩堂。
English: That's not necessary, Nick.

Japanese: そんなモノ見せなくたって、 知ってるコトはしゃべるから。
English: You don't have to threaten me. I'll tell you what I know.

Japanese: こう見えてオレ、ちゃんと 反省してるからな。
English: I know it's tough to tell, but I'm really sorry about what happened...

Japanese: （こんなスナオな矢張、 見たことないぞ‥‥）
English: Wow, I've never seen Larry look so serious. I actually believe him...

Japanese: ホント。まるで、ベツジンだね‥‥
English: Yeah... He's like a totally different person...

Japanese: なんだったら、 名刺交換でもしておくか？
English: In that case, maybe you and I should get reacquainted.

Japanese: ‥‥これ、倉院の里の 秘宝だけど‥‥
English: This is one of Kurain's greatest treasures.

Japanese: オレ、こういうジミなヤツって キョーミなかったんだけど‥‥
English: I used to have zero interest in all of this old, junky stuff...

Japanese: ココロを入れかえて見ると、 なんて言うか、アジがあるよな。
English: But, now that I'm a new person, well... I can kinda see the appeal.

Japanese: そうだ！　こんど、お寺めぐり でもしねえか、成歩堂！
English: Hey! I got it! Why don't we all go visit one of those musty old temples next time?

Japanese: ‥‥ヤッパリさん、なんか 好感度がアップしたよ。
English: ...I think I could actually learn to like this new Larry.

Japanese: ‥‥あいかわらず、役に立つ 情報はくれないけど、ね。
English: Yeah, but he still hasn't told us anything useful.

Japanese: ‥‥あのな。 よく聞いてくれよ、成歩堂。
English: Umm... Listen carefully, OK, Nick?

Japanese: たしかにオレ、 イイカゲンなオトコだけど‥‥
English: I know you don't think much of me as a person...

Japanese: ドロボーはしないって。 まして、怪人なんてさあ！
English: But I'm no thief. And I would never wear a goofy getup like that!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ダレも、おまえが 怪人だなんて言ってないよ。
English: Umm... Nobody ever accused you of being a thief.

Japanese: めんどくせえだろ。 計画立てて、予告状書いて。
English: It's too much work for me. Making plans, writing calling cards, etc., etc...

Japanese: ラーメン食い逃げした方が ラクでいいって。
English: I'd rather just pull a dine and dash at a burger joint.

Japanese: あ。あたし、ラーメン 大好きですよ！
English: Burgers!? I love burgers, too!

Japanese: お。マヨイちゃん、ホント？ じゃ、どうよ？　今晩あたり。
English: Really? Well, how about it? Wanna go on a burger date tonight?

Japanese: ‥‥ちゃんと、カネ払えよ。
English: ...At least pay for the food, because I'm not defending you again.

Japanese: あの。これなんですけど‥‥
English: Umm, so what about this...?

Japanese: え！　マヨイちゃん、 もしかして、ら、ラブレター‥‥
English: Huh!? Maya, you shouldn't have! A love letter for me!?

Japanese: いえ、その‥‥ 脅迫状なんですけど‥‥
English: Uh, no. Sorry. This is a blackmail letter.

Japanese: えええええッ！　マヨイちゃん、 なんで知ってるんだよ！
English: Whaaaaat!? How do you know about that, Maya!?
Huh?

Japanese: エミコのことだろ！ い、いくらほしいんだよ！
English: How much is Alexis demanding this time!?

Japanese: ‥‥ダレだよ、エミコって‥‥
English: Huh? Who is this "Alexis"?

Japanese: たしか、きのうも見せたのに。 この脅迫状‥‥
English: Didn't we show him this letter yesterday, too? I'm pretty sure we did...

Japanese: あ！　オレが拾った サイフじゃねえか！
English: Ah! That's the wallet I found!

Japanese: おシゴトをサボって、殴られに 行ったときに拾ったんですよね！
English: You found it when you snuck out of work to get socked in the kisser, right?

Japanese: ‥‥ベツに殴られに行った ワケじゃねえんだケド。
English: ...I didn't "sneak out of work to get socked in the kisser".

Japanese: もし、あの晩オレが マジメにはたらいていたら‥‥
English: If only I had been more responsible at work...

Japanese: ハンニンを目撃できた かもしれないんだよな‥‥。
English: I might have spotted the murderer on the monitor like I was supposed to...

Japanese: ヤッパリさん‥‥ やっぱり、気にしてるんだ。
English: Larry... You really do feel bad, don't you?

Japanese: ま、いいか。
English: Yeah, well...

Japanese: そのかわり、マレカちゃんと 知り合えたワケだし。
English: Maybe it's all for the best. ...I think it's brought Dessie and I closer together.

Japanese: 人生、ナニゴトも “プラス思考”で行かねえとな。
English: Just like they say, "Every cloud has a silver lining".

Japanese: ドンマイ、ミスタ・ヤハーリ！ ‥‥なーんて。
English: "You gotta see that old water glass as half-full". Am I right...?

Japanese: （‥‥まあ、こっちのほうが 矢張らしいかな）
English: (Now he's starting to sound like the Larry I know.)

Japanese: お。なるほどくんも “ぷらす思考”だね！
English: Don't forget, Nick! Half-full!

Japanese: これ、犯人が使ったカード‥‥ なのかな。
English: So was this the card that was used by the culprit that night...?

Japanese: シリアル番号を見ると、 そういうコトになるかなあ。
English: Well, going off the serial number, then yeah.

Japanese: 午前１時に社長室のドアを 開けたの、そのカードだぜ。
English: This card was used to open the CEO's office door at 1:00 in the morning.

Japanese: オマエの依頼人が持ってたんだろ？ ハンニンだな、そいつ。
English: Your client had this card, right? Then it's a no-brainer. He's the killer, man.

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Nnngh...

Japanese: あの‥‥社長室のブザーって、 ここにつながってるんですよね？
English: Umm... The buzzer in the CEO's office is directly connected to this room, right?

Japanese: そうさ！　オレとマヨイちゃんの ココロみたいだよな！
English: That's right! Just like my heart is connected to yours, Maya!

Japanese: ‥‥はい？
English: ...Huh?

Japanese: マヨイちゃんがココロの ブザーを押してくれれば！
English: Go ahead, Maya. Press the buzzer in your heart!

Japanese: オレ、どこへだって飛んでいくぜ！ ‥‥プロのガードマンとして。
English: I promise I'll come running to your rescue, like the professional guard I am!

Japanese: へええ。‥‥スゴいんですねえ。
English: Wow... That was pretty good.

Japanese: そうさ。スゴいんだぜオレは！
English: Heh heh, thanks. I try!

Japanese: ‥‥このブザーのコト、 聞かせてほしいんだけど。
English: Do you think you could tell us about the buzzer now?

Japanese: うん。聞きたい聞きたい！
English: Yes, please! Please tell us!

Japanese: ううん‥‥しゃアねえなあ、もお。 詳しく話しちゃおうかなァ！
English: OK... I guess so. Prepare to be wowed!

Japanese: ‥‥オレときたら、反省した瞬間、 もう次のネタが出てきやがる。
English: You guys have really made me reflect on the way I've been living...

Japanese: トラブルに巻きこまれて、 いちいち反省していたら、
English: But, the only problem is, with all this reflecting...

Japanese: 恋するヒマもありゃしねえ。 ‥‥やめちまうか、ハンセイ。
English: Where is the time for love? Frankly, I think reflecting is a bit overrated, you know?

Japanese: な、ナニ言ってるんですか、 ヤッパリさん！
English: Wh-What are you going on about, Larry!?

Japanese: それで‥‥ケッキョク、 この記録について、何か情報は？
English: Plus, do you honestly have any new info on the buzzer record?

Japanese: ん？　いや。ないよ。ベツに。
English: Huh? The record? Ah, no... Nothing at all.

Japanese: （‥‥こいつの“反省”って どのテイドのものなんだろ‥‥）
English: (This guy reflects about as much as a piece of black carpeting...)

Japanese: うーん‥‥反省したオレとしては、 役に立ちたいのはヤマヤマだぜ。
English: Umm... I think you know how much I want to help you guys, but...

Japanese: でもなあ‥‥ワルいけど、 ソイツのことは、わからねえな。
English: I really don't know what to say about that.

Japanese: どうしたの？　なるほどくん。
English: What is it, Nick?

Japanese: い、いや。今日の矢張は、 ちょっといいヤツに思えてさ。
English: It's just that he seems so... I don't know... "pleasant".

Japanese: オマエ、もう２６才だろ。
English: You're 26 years old, right?

Japanese: そろそろ、しっかり生き方を 見つめなおしたほうがいいぜ！
English: Don't you think it's about time you took a long, hard look at your life?

Japanese: は、はあ。
English: Uhh, sure.

Japanese: 若いうちだけだぞ。 デカいシッパイができるのは。
English: The only time you can really screw up and hope to recover from it is when you're young!

Japanese: そんなモノですかあ‥‥
English: Huh? Well, I guess so...

Japanese: あ！　もちろん、 マヨイちゃんはベツだぜ！
English: Naturally I'm not talking about you, Maya! You're already perfect!

Japanese: （デカいシッパイをしない 矢張なんて、想像できないけど）
English: (It's hard to imagine Larry not screwing up at any age.)

Japanese: お！　おぼえてるぜ！ マヨイちゃんのネエちゃんだろ！
English: Hey, I remember her! That's Maya's big sister!

Japanese: わあ！　おぼえてて くれたんですか！
English: Wow! You really do remember!

Japanese: 美女よなあ。姉妹そろってさあ！
English: Yeah, who could forget! A couple of gorgeous sisters! Hubba hubba!

Japanese: ビジョだって、なるほどくん！
English: You hear that, Nick! Gorgeous!

Japanese: （そういえば、 矢張を弁護したとき‥‥）
English: (Oh yeah... That's right. When I defended Larry...)

Japanese: （千尋さん、まだ 生きていたんだっけな‥‥）
English: (Mia was still alive...)

Japanese: しばらく会わないうちに グッとかわいくなったね！
English: Wow! You've gotten even cuter than the last time I saw you.

Japanese: え！　ホントですか！
English: Oh, really!?

Japanese: もお、２年前とはベツジンだぜ。 なあ、成歩堂！
English: Yeah, you look totally different than two years ago. Don't you think, Nick?

Japanese: （近くにいるせいか、あんまり 変わったようには見えないな）
English: (Maybe it's 'cause we're always together, but she looks pretty much the same to me.)

Japanese: オレたち、こういうジュンスイな ヒトミを忘れちゃダメだよな！
English: Seeing her makes me remember how important it is to stay young at heart!

Japanese: コドモのころの、きれいなキモチ ‥‥オレはなくしたくないぜ。
English: Even when you grow up, you gotta keep the part of your heart that's still pure...

Japanese: ホント、そうですよねー‥‥
English: You're so right about that!

Japanese: その“きれいなキモチ”を 持っていたコドモのころ‥‥
English: I'm impressed that you can still think like that, Larry.

Japanese: おまえにはヒドい目に あわされたっけな。‥‥矢張。
English: After all, you went through some pretty tough times as a kid...

Japanese: アレ。そうだっけ？
English: Huh? I did?

Japanese: （きれいなココロもそうでない ココロも、サッパリ忘れてるな）
English: (Something tells me it's more about self-induced amnesia than having a pure heart...)

Japanese: あ。これ、刑事だろ。 さっき、いろいろ聞かれたぜ。
English: Oh, this guy? The detective? He was here before, drilling me with a ton of questions.

Japanese: へえ‥‥ どんなコトを話したんですか？
English: Really? Like, what did he ask?

Japanese: えーと‥‥‥‥‥‥ もう、忘れちまった。
English: Um, let's see... I can't remember.

Japanese: あれ。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ま。そういった、きわめて どうでもいいコトを話したワケ。
English: Well, you know... just a bunch of boring stuff.

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事もキノドクに‥‥）
English: (Poor Detective Gumshoe...)

Japanese: いやー、やっぱり イイよなあ、マレカちゃん。
English: That Dessie is one awesome lady alright!

Japanese: ひさしぶりに、マジで ホレちまいそうだぜ。
English: It's been a long time since I've felt this way... I think I'm really in love this time!

Japanese: ひさしぶり、ですかあ‥‥
English: A long time, huh?

Japanese: あ！　モチロン、 マヨイちゃんはベツよ！
English: Yeah! Well, err... Except for what I feel for you, Maya!

Japanese: （本当に反省してるのか わからないな、コイツは）
English: (I don't think even HE knows what he's saying anymore.)

Japanese: オレも、２５才なんだよなー。 そろそろ、考えないと。
English: I'm 25 now. It's about time I started giving some serious thought to my life.

Japanese: そうだよな。 ‥‥って、何を考えるんだ？
English: Good! That's the way to be! ...What exactly are you thinking about, though?

Japanese: そりゃオマエ。 将来のユメ、とか。
English: Well, you know... My future, my dreams...

Japanese: ‥‥あの。ヤッパリさんにとって、 “将来”って、いつから‥‥？
English: Larry, when exactly does this "future" of yours start?

Japanese: オトコは３０才にして立ち上がる！ ‥‥オレの持論さ。
English: A man's life begins at 30! ...That's my theory anyway.

Japanese: 今度のシッパイをかみしめて、 あと５年間、じっくり遊ぶぜ！
English: I figure I got another 5 years of guilt-free screw-ups before I have to buckle down.

Japanese: ‥‥矢張。今度、 ゆっくり語り合おうか。
English: Larry, I think... Forget it. We're just gonna have to have a nice long talk next time...

Japanese: ウチのシャチョーだよ。 なかなかイイ人だったぜ。
English: The CEO of our company. He was a pretty good guy.

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。そうなんですか？
English: Huh? Really?

Japanese: ホラ。オレって、マジメに はたらくのがニガテなオトコだろ？
English: Yeah, you bet! You know how I'm not very good about working hard, right?

Japanese: ‥‥ヒトに同意を求めるなよ。
English: Uh, yeah, I kinda noticed...

Japanese: シャチョーは、そんなオレに こう言ってくれたんだよ。
English: Well, guess what the Boss said to me about that?

Japanese: 『シゴトはテキトーでいい。 そのかわり、給料もテキトーだ』
English: "I don't expect much work from you, Larry. In return, just don't expect much pay."

Japanese: はあ‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: なんか、法律ができたらしいぜ。 ビルに警備員を置け、みたいな。
English: I guess he had to follow some kind of dumb law about having security here in the building.

Japanese: （だから、大安売りのアルバイトを やとったワケか‥‥）
English: (So, in order to pay the lowest wage possible, he went to the bottom of the barrel.)

Japanese: シャチョーは、オレの アコガレの人だったんだ。
English: To be honest, I really looked up to the Boss.

Japanese: いつかオレもシャチョーになって、 テキトーにやりたいぜ！
English: If I ever grow up to be a CEO, I wanna be just like him!

Japanese: そのとき、警備員はモチロン オマエだぜ、成歩堂！
English: And don't worry, Nick! I promise I'll hire you for security!

Japanese: うおおおおお！ ダレよコレ！　ダレよ成歩堂！
English: WHOA! WHO is this! Come on, man, don't hold out on me!

Japanese: い、いやいや。 ‥‥知らなきゃいいよ、ベツに。
English: Forget about it. You don't need to know and I'm not telling you.

Japanese: よくねぇよ、成歩堂！ オマエだって知ってるだろ！
English: Hey now! I thought we were pals!

Japanese: オレ、黒いノースリーブでメガネの オンナに、めっぽうヨワいんだよ！
English: I got a MAJOR thing for girls with black sleeveless shirts and glasses!

Japanese: そんなの知るかよ！
English: How do you know that!?

Japanese: これで、オレンジ色の手帳でも 持っていたら、も。たまんねえぜ！
English: And if they have an orange- colored memo pad, I really lose my cool!

Japanese: （‥‥本当に知らないのか？ 霧緒さんのこと‥‥）
English: (He has to be making that up... He can't really know Adrian... Can he...?)

Japanese: ホント、モウシワケない成歩堂！ 反省してるんだぜ、オレ。
English: I'm really sorry, Nick! I swear I want to change my ways.

Japanese: でもな。オレ、やっぱり オトコにキョーミないんだわ。
English: But the thing is... I just don't have any interest in men.

Japanese: （反省しても、そこだけは 変わらないんだな‥‥）
English: (This guy really needs to get his mind out of the gutter...)

Japanese: オトコらしいねー、ヤッパリさん。
English: Larry's your stereotypical guy, alright.

Japanese: ～事件当夜のこと～
English: -- The Night of the Crime --

Japanese: 事件があった晩‥‥おまえ、 キチンと仕事してたのか？
English: On the night of the crime, were you working hard like you were supposed to?

Japanese: あ、あた、あたり、あたりま、 あたりまえだろうが！
English: What? Huh? Of-Of-Of Of course I was! Why wouldn't I have been?

Japanese: きのうはサボって、まれかさんに 会いに来てたじゃないか。
English: But didn't you sneak out of work just yesterday to go see Ms. DeLite?

Japanese: う‥‥！　で、でも！ それはそれ。これはこれ、だぜ。
English: Ah...! B-But that was that... and-and this is this!

Japanese: もしかして、おまえ。 あの晩も、サボってたんじゃあ‥‥
English: Is there any chance that you snuck out of work last night, too...?

Japanese: サボってねえって！　１００円 までなら、かけてもいいぜ！
English: Never! I didn't sneak out! I tell you what! I'll even bet you a dollar!

Japanese: （マゴコロの大安売りだな‥‥）
English: (A dollar, wow! Now that's confidence!)

Japanese: なんだよ！　その、小雨にぬれ そぼった子犬を見るような目は！
English: What's with that drenched in the rain puppy look on your face!?

Japanese: オレがあの晩、持ち場をはなれた っていう、証拠でもあるのかよ！
English: Do you have evidence that I left my position or are you just pulling my chain!?

Japanese: （事件の起こった時間に、矢張が 警備室にいなかった証拠は‥‥）
English: (The evidence that Larry was not manning his station when the murder happened is...)

Japanese: ふう‥‥アブねえアブねえ。
English: Whew... I was scared for a minute...

Japanese: （矢張のヤツ‥‥ロコツに ホッとしてるぞ‥‥）
English: (Hmmm... Larry seems pretty relieved...)

Japanese: ホッとなんかしてねえって！ ７０円までなら、かけてもいいぜ！
English: But I tell you I'm not relieved! I'll even bet you 70 cents!

Japanese: （‥‥ゼッタイ解除してやるぞ、 そのロック‥‥）
English: (I'm going to break this Psyche-Lock if it's the last thing I do!)

Japanese: なんだよ！　その、エサを横どり された小ザルみたいな目は！
English: What's with that baby who had his favorite toy taken away from him look on your face?

Japanese: これ、社長室のキーカード なんだってな。
English: This is the key card to the CEO's office, right?

Japanese: そ、そうだケド。
English: Y-Yeah, that's right.

Japanese: キーカードは、 サイフの中から発見された。
English: This key card was found inside a wallet.

Japanese: このサイフ‥‥ モチロン知ってるよな？
English: This wallet... You know about this, right?

Japanese: 見たコトもねえぜ。
English: I've never seen it before.

Japanese: ウソつけ！　きのう、おまえが 届けに来たんじゃないか！
English: Liar! You hand delivered this wallet to Ms. DeLite just yesterday!

Japanese: くそ。つまらねえコト おぼえてやがるなー。
English: Gimme a break! You can't expect me to remember every little thing that happens!

Japanese: ‥‥さすがに、きのうのコト ぐらい、おぼえているよ。
English: Well, I do expect you to remember something that happened just yesterday!

Japanese: おまえがコレを拾ったの ‥‥何時ごろだ？
English: What time was it when you found this wallet?

Japanese: たしか‥‥１時ごろかな。夜中の。 バイト先の、ビルの１階でさ。
English: I guess it was around 1:00 in the morning on the first floor of our company building.

Japanese: 夜中の‥‥１時‥‥？
English: 1 o'clock... In the morning...?

Japanese: ‥‥そういうコトだ。 つまり、矢張！
English: That's right. In other words, Larry...

Japanese: おまえは事件が起こったとき、 警備室にはいなかったんだよ！
English: At the time the of the murder, you were away from the security guard office!

Japanese: うわがががああアァッ！
English: AAAAAAAAH!!

Japanese: で、でも！　でもでも！ な、なんていうか、アレだぜ！
English: Y-Y-Yeah, B-B-But! th-there's something you didn't think about!

Japanese: なんだよ？
English: What's that?

Japanese: あの日、オレの警備担当は 夜の１０時からだったんだけどさ。
English: My shift that day didn't start until 10:00 PM!

Japanese: ハンニンは、それより前に 現場に侵入してたかもしれないぜ！
English: The murderer might have snuck in before then!

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことだ？
English: ...What do you mean by that?

Japanese: もし、ハンニンが１０時より 前に侵入していたら、
English: If the murderer had snuck in before 10:00 PM...

Japanese: それ、オレのセキニンじゃねえよ。 前のヤツが見落としたんだからな！
English: ...then it wasn't my fault! It was the fault of the guy whose shift was before mine!

Japanese: （どうも、まだコトの重大さが わかってないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Why do I have the feeling that he still doesn't get the seriousness of this...?)

Japanese: ‥‥いいか、矢張。
English: Listen up, Larry.

Japanese: あの晩、殺人犯がＫＢ警備の 社長室に侵入したのは‥‥
English: We know beyond a shadow of a doubt that...

Japanese: まちがいなく、おまえの担当だった 午後１０時より“あと”なんだよ！
English: ...the killer "snuck" into the CEO's office after 10:00 PM, during your shift!

Japanese: ふう‥‥まったく、 ヒヤヒヤさせやがって！
English: Whew... Boy, you really had me scared!

Japanese: （コイツ‥‥ココロの底から ホッとしてるぞ‥‥）
English: (I've never seen anyone look so relieved in my whole life...)

Japanese: なんだよ！　その、眠れぬ夜の 子ヒツジみたいな目は！
English: What's with that little lamb that can't get to sleep look on your face?

Japanese: （‥‥なんとしても 思い知らせてやらないとな‥‥）
English: (I guess I'm gonna have to rattle his cage a little bit more...)

Japanese: 矢張。このキーカード‥‥ 使うと、記録が残るんだよな？
English: Larry... When you use this key card, does it leave a record?

Japanese: ああ、そうだぜ。‥‥まあ、 部外者には見せられないけどな。
English: Yeah, it does. But I can't show the record to just anyone you know...

Japanese: その記録‥‥今日の法廷で 公表されたんだよ。
English: That key card data was already made public in the trial today.

Japanese: なんだよそれ！ 聞いてねえぞ、オレ。
English: What!? I didn't know that!

Japanese: そういうの、オレを通して もらわないとコマるんだよなー。
English: Any kind of request for info like that is supposed to go through me!

Japanese: （エラそうに言うなよ‥‥）
English: (Boy does that sound a bit arrogant coming from a part-time guard...)

Japanese: とにかく、その記録によると、 まさに犯行時刻の午前１時‥‥
English: Anyway, according to the data, the door to the CEO's office was opened with this card...

Japanese: 社長室のロックが、このカードで 解除されているんだよ。
English: Furthermore, it was most definitely used at 1 AM, the time of the murder!

Japanese: ウソだッ！　そんなの！
English: No way!

Japanese: コイツを使って、だれかが 社長室に侵入したんだ。
English: Yes, someone used this to get into the CEO's office.

Japanese: その時間は、事件当夜の午前１時！ どう考えても、おまえの担当だ！
English: That happened at 1 AM on the night of the crime. Right in the middle of your shift!

Japanese: ううううう‥‥
English: Ooooh...

Japanese: 矢張！　これで、 セキニン逃れはできないぞ！
English: Larry, you can't duck your responsibility this time!

Japanese: うわああああああああああああッ！
English: GYYYYYAAAAAAR! NOOO!
